faults.description: Confirm signal absent from Taxi-to-Driver API. The central sends Confirm to the taxi module (3.4.1) after a driver-submitted order, but 3.2.2 defines no Confirm signal for the taxi module to forward this to the driver.

No signal defined to notify the taxi that no cars are available at the requested time. Variant 4b requires this notification so the driver can resubmit with a different time, but no such signal exists in the Central-to-Taxi API.
Order_struct does not contain a priority field. Task 2 states that orders can be prioritized, but the data structure provides no attribute to carry priority information.

No signal defined for the system to present a time estimate to the operator when an order is very close in time. Variant 3b requires this information but no corresponding Central-to-Operator signal exists.
LoginSignal carries no parameters. Task 3 requires the terminal to send both driver information and position to the central, but LoginSignal has no fields for driver ID or position.
LoggedIn(integer) contains only the taxi number. Tasks 3 requires driver information to be sent to the central, but no driver ID parameter is present in the LoggedIn signal.

LoginFailed signal is absent from Central-to-Taxi API. Variant 4b requires the central to reject an invalid card and keep the car in Offline state, but no LoginFailed signal exists in 3.4.1 to communicate this rejection to the taxi module.

ZoneInfo signal not defined in Central-to-Taxi API (3.4.1). Task 6 requires the central to proactively send zone overview information to the car after login, but no mechanism exists for the central to push ZoneInfo to a taxi.

No signal from Central to Taxi for automatic order cancellation after 2-minute timeout. Variant 3b requires the order to be canceled automatically if the driver neither accepts nor rejects within 2 minutes, but no cancellation notification signal is defined.

MSC 4.1 is missing a Central-to-Taxi Confirm step. After step 8 (Central receives Ack), the design shows no Confirm signal sent back to the taxi, yet Task 4 requires the central to confirm the accepted order to the driver.

faults.use_case: 1.5 Taxi: Receive order / 1.1 Taxi: Submit order
faults.description: Confirm signal is not present in the communication protocol table (Table 1). Since Central sends Confirm to Taxi over the radio link (3.4.1), it must be encoded in the protocol, but no Confirm message type is defined.

faults.use_case: 1.7 Central: Handle incoming alarms / 1.8 Taxi: Alarm event
faults.description: No alarm trigger signal defined in Taxi-to-Central API. Tasks 1-2 of UC 1.8 require the taxi to alert the central when the driver activates the alarm, but 3.4.2 defines no alarm notification signal.

faults.use_case: 1.5 Taxi: Receive order / 1.6 Taxi: Driving a customer
faults.description: No signal for the Waiting-for-Customer state. After accepting an order (Task 6 of UC 1.5), the car must enter Waiting-for-Customer state, but no signal communicates this state change from taxi to central.

Position(integer, integer) is missing a time parameter. Task 8 requires the driver to send both the arrival zone and expected arrival time to the central when entering Soon-Available state, but Position only carries taxi number and zone number.

Position signal transmits zone number, not exact coordinates. Task 3 requires exact coordinates to be sent to the central every 30 seconds during an alarm event, but the Position signal provides only a coarse zone identifier.

ZoneInfo missing from Central-to-Taxi API. Task 2 requires the central to send overview information to the taxi terminal in response to an Overview request, but no ZoneInfo signal is defined in 3.4.1.

No signal to notify the operator when a car rejects a manually dispatched order. Variant 4b requires the operator to be informed so the order can be dispatched to another car, but no rejection notification signal is defined in 3.3.2.
No text message acknowledgment signal from Taxi to Central. Task 4 requires each receiving car to automatically acknowledge receipt, but no such signal is defined in the Taxi-to-Central API.

No operator notification signal for failed text message delivery to a specific car. Variant 4b requires the operator to be notified when a message cannot reach its destination, but no such signal exists in 3.3.2.

faults.use_case: 1.12 Central: Log in to central system
faults.description: No operator login signal defined in the Central-Operator API. UC 1.12 requires operators to authenticate with user ID and password, but 3.3.1 contains no signal for submitting login credentials.

Voice_msg in 3.4.2 has parameters (taxi number, voice data) – 2 parameters – but MSC 4.2 shows Voice_Msg sent with 3 parameters (Operator_Nbr, Taxi_Nbr, Voice_Data). The operator number is missing from the signal definition in 3.4.2.

No signal for the driver to request car/driver properties (3.2.1) and no signal from Central to Taxi to deliver the requested properties (3.4.1). Both a request and a response signal are required by UC 1.16 but neither is defined.
No signal for updating driver properties in the Central-Operator API. UC 1.18 requires the operator to select a driver and submit updated properties, but 3.3.1 defines no such signal.
